author: parithosh
content:
We have the ability to mess with the network built into kurtosis now, no need for kubernetes anymore! There's a new tool called [disruptoor](https://github.com/ethpandaops/disruptoor), heavily inspired by work from csaba's [ethereum-kurtosis-tc](https://github.com/cskiraly/ethereum-kurtosis-tc) and tyler's [kurtosis-chaos](https://github.com/protocol-security/kurtosis-chaos). The main difference is that we've upstreamed all the changes with disruptoor into kurtosis and it should just work without any changes to docker or kubernetes, using the usual assertoor workflow you know and love. 

examples:
1. Reorgs of depth 10:
```
... add your config here ....
additional_services:
  - assertoor
  - disruptoor
  - dora

assertoor_params:
  tests:
    - file: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ethpandaops/assertoor/refs/heads/master/playbooks/dev/two-way-network-split-reorg-trigger.yaml"
      config:
        splitDurationSlots: 10
```
2. Split the network, trigger non-finality, re-join the network and check for finality:
```
... add your config here ....
additional_services:
  - assertoor
  - disruptoor
  - dora

assertoor_params:
  tests:
    - file: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ethpandaops/assertoor/refs/heads/master/playbooks/dev/two-way-network-split-non-finality.yaml"
```
